title: A reinforced and fire-resistant pole
abstract: A wooden utility pole 10, wherein a ground wire 22 extends along a substantial length of an upright 12 of the pole 10, and wherein the upright 12 is provided with one or more layers of a fibreglass fabric 14 that has been impregnated with a fire- resistant resin prior to being applied to an outer surface of the upright 12, thereby 5 overlaying and covering a substantial portion of the ground wire 22.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A wooden utility pole wherein at least a portion of an upright of the pole is provided with one or more layers of a fibreglass fabric that has been impregnated with a fire-resistant resin prior to being applied to an outer surface of the upright. 2. A pole as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the portion of the upright provided with the impregnated fibreglass fabric in use spans a zone located immediately above and below a soil surface. 3. A pole as claimed in claim 2, wherein the length of the zone to which the impregnated fibreglass fabric is applied is about 100 cm so that, in use, 50 cm of the impregnated fibreglass fabric is located above the soil surface and 50 cm is located below the soil surface. 4. A pole as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein cross arm of the pole is provided with one or more layers of the fibreglass fabric that has been impregnated with the fire-resistant resin prior to being applied to an outer surface of the cross arm. 5. A pole as claimed in claim 4, wherein, in addition, the entire upright is provided with impregnated fibreglass fabric. 6. A wooden utility pole, wherein a ground wire and/or an electrically insulated conducting wire extends along a substantial length of an upright of the pole, and wherein the upright is provided with one or more layers of a fibreglass fabric that has been impregnated with a fire-resistant resin prior to being applied to an outer surface of the upright, thereby overlaying and covering a substantial portion of the wire. 7. A pole as claimed in claim 6, wherein the portion of the upright provided with the impregnated fibreglass fabric in use spans a zone located immediately below a soil surface and a zone located below a cross arm of the pole. 8. A pole as claimed in claim 6 or claim 7, wherein the ground wire and/or an electrically insulated conducting wire extends beyond the portion of the upright provided with the impregnated fibreglass fabric to facilitate electrical 

 connection of the wire at a first end thereof closest to the cross arm and to facilitate grounding or electrical connection thereof at its second end. 9. A pole as claimed in any one of claims 6 to 8, wherein a cross arm of the utility pole is provided with one or more layers of the fibreglass fabric that has been impregnated with the fire-resistant resin prior to being applied to an outer surface of the cross arm. 10. A pole as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least two layers of impregnated fibreglass fabric are applied to the upright and/or cross arm. 1 1 . A pole as claimed in claim 10, wherein, if is it desired to further increase the rigidity of the portion around which the impregnated fibreglass fabric is applied, additional layers are added. 12. A pole as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the fibreglass fabric is a woven roving fibreglass fabric. 13. A pole as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the resin is a high tensile composite resin that includes a fire retardant. 14. A pole as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the fibreglass fabric is passed through a resin bath to impregnate it prior to being wrapped around the upright and/ or the cross arm after which the resin is allowed to set and cool down. 15. A pole as claimed in claim 14, wherein the resin bath is configured to control the resin content of the impregnated fibreglass fabric. 16. A method of providing a wooden utility pole with a zone of impregnated fibreglass fabric, the method including at least the steps of:- - passing fibreglass fabric through a resin bath to impregnate it with resin; and - wrapping the impregnated fibreglass fabric around one or more portions of the utility pole until a desired thickness of impregnated fibreglass fabric is achieved. 17. A method as claimed in claim 16, wherein the impregnated fibreglass fabric is a woven roving fibreglass fabric and the resin is a high tensile composite resin including a fire retardant. 18. A method as claimed in claim 16 or claim 18, wherein at least one layer of impregnated fibreglass fabric is wrapped around the portion(s) of the pole. 19. A method as claimed in any one of claims 16 to 18, wherein the method is carried out while the pole is in an operative condition. 20. A wooden utility pole according to the invention, as hereinbefore generally described. 21 . A wooden utility pole as specifically described with reference to or as illustrated in any one of the accompanying drawings. 22. A wooden utility pole including any new and inventive integer or combination of integers, substantially as herein described. 23. A method according to the invention for providing a wooden utility pole with a zone of impregnated fibreglass fabric substantially as hereinbefore described or exemplified. 24. A method of providing a wooden utility pole with a zone of impregnated fibreglass fabric including any new and inventive integer or combination of integers, substantially as herein described.
